qa_pairs.question: When will invitation emails for Fast Track applications be sent?
qa_pairs.answer: Invitation emails for eligible PIs will be sent in mid-September 2025.

qa_pairs.question: When does the CCDB open for Fast Track submissions?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCDB will open for Fast Track submissions on September 23, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting Fast Track applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The deadline to submit Fast Track applications is November 4, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Can the Fast Track application deadline be extended?
qa_pairs.answer: No, extending this deadline is not possible.

qa_pairs.question: When will PIs be notified of the Fast Track competition results?
qa_pairs.answer: Notifications with the results of the competition will be sent to PIs in late March 2026.

qa_pairs.question: When will new allocations be implemented for Fast Track applicants?
qa_pairs.answer: New allocations will be implemented in early April 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs with an existing RRG award who meet the Fast Track eligibility are allowed to submit a lightweight progress report to renew their request for computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: Does applying through Fast Track guarantee the same allocation as the previous year?
qa_pairs.answer: No, applying through the Fast Track process does not guarantee that you will receive the same allocation as last year.

qa_pairs.question: How is the resource request scaled in the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: The score received in your original RRG application will be reused, and your resource request will be scaled using the same function as all other applications, according to demand and supply.

qa_pairs.question: What is the science score requirement for Fast Track eligibility?
qa_pairs.answer: To use the Fast Track process, the previous RRG application must have received a science score greater than 2.0 points (out of 5).

qa_pairs.question: For how many consecutive years can the Fast Track process be used for a given application?
qa_pairs.answer: This process can be used for no more than two consecutive years for a given application.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I believe I am eligible for Fast Track but didn't receive an invitation?
qa_pairs.answer: If you believe you meet the eligibility criteria but did not receive an invitation to Fast Track, you should email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What are the benefits of the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: The benefits of the Fast Track process include a lightweight application, no additional peer-review, and the ability to apply for up to two consecutive years.

qa_pairs.question: Do Fast Track applications undergo a scientific review?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Fast Track applications receive a technical review but do not receive another scientific review.

qa_pairs.question: How do eligible PIs receive their Fast Track invitation?
qa_pairs.answer: Eligible PIs will receive an email with an invitation to Fast Track in September.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Fast Track application form?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fast Track application form will show by default on CCDB at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_competitions/applications.

qa_pairs.question: How do I start working on a Fast Track application on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: To start working on a Fast Track application, click on 'Edit' and then 'Submit' when you are ready.

qa_pairs.question: What if I want to submit a full RRG application instead of a Fast Track application?
qa_pairs.answer: To decline the invitation to Fast Track and submit a full RRG application instead, click on 'Change to RRG'.

qa_pairs.question: If I switch from Fast Track to RRG, can I change my mind later?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you change your mind later on, you can click on 'Change to Fast Track' to continue working on your Fast Track application, and any information you entered will be saved.

qa_pairs.question: Can I delegate access to my Fast Track application to another user?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can click on 'Delegate' to grant access to another user with an active account with the Alliance, to the application you wish to submit.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for an application if it is delegated to another user?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI remains responsible for the application submitted and the utilization of the computational resources allocated, even if a delegated user submits it on their behalf.

qa_pairs.question: What information is required in the 'Details' section of the Fast Track 2026 application form?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Details' section requires sufficient justification for any change requested compared to the eligible amount of resources communicated in the invitation email, and a description of any issues with your allocation, or 'No issue to report'.

qa_pairs.question: What types of modifications to current allocations are allowed in a Fast Track application?
qa_pairs.answer: Allowed modifications include a change of resource location, a 10% increase in cloud compute and storage resources, and a 10% increase of PROJECT, NEARLINE and/or dCache storage resources.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a maximum limit for increasing PROJECT, NEARLINE, or dCache storage resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a 10% increase of PROJECT, NEARLINE and/or dCache storage resources is allowed, up to a maximum of 50 TB of additional storage based on the eligible amount communicated in the invitation email.

qa_pairs.question: Can I request an increase in CPU or GPU resources through the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: No, an increase of CPU or GPU requests is not allowed; you may only request up to the amount mentioned in the invitation email.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I need more CPU or GPU compute resources than what is allowed in Fast Track?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need more compute resources (CPU or GPU) than allowed, you must submit a new application.

qa_pairs.question: What should be included in the 'Progress report' section of the Fast Track application?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Progress report' section should provide details about what has been accomplished with the resources used, such as publications.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have questions about the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions about the Fast Track process, you should email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is a 'lightweight progress report' in the context of the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: A lightweight progress report is what PIs with existing RRG awards who meet Fast Track eligibility can submit to renew their request for computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: What could happen to my allocated resources if I apply through the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: The resulting allocation may be scaled by a different factor than last year, which could lead to either an increase or a decrease in allocated resources for your project compared to last year.
